title: The U.S. and China agree to temporarily reduce tariffs
bodyHtml:
The United States and China issued a joint trade statement for the first time in many years following negotiations held this weekend in Geneva, Switzerland.

Both sides will reduce the additional tariffs they impose on each other by 115%. The U.S. will lower its total additional tariff on China from 145% (composed of 20% fentanyl tariffs and 125% reciprocal tariffs) to 30% (20% fentanyl + 10% reciprocal tariff) for a period of 90 days.

China, in turn, will reduce its 125% additional tariff on the U.S. to 10% for 90 days.

Both parties will implement these adjustments by May 14.

However, the U.S. will continue to impose Section 301, Section 232, and fentanyl-related tariffs on China.

Please click for the related factsheet issued by the White House .
